content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

Determine the count of subarrays of AAA which contain their length as an element.

Formally, count the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N)(1\le L\le R\le N)(1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N) such that: (R‚àíL+1)‚àà{AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR}(R-L+1) \in \{A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_R\}(R‚àíL+1)‚àà{AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã}.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains an integer NNN denoting the length of the array AAA.
Second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã - denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the count of subarrays containing their lengths.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105.
